sentence: La luz del sol reluce sobre'l valle.
ipa: la ˈluθ del ˈsol reˈluθe ˈsobɾe ˈl ˈbaje
gloss_en: The sunlight shines over the valley.
notes: Distinción preserved: ⟨z⟩ and ⟨c⟩ before front vowel [θ] 'luz','reluce' per penny2002; yeísta ⟨ll⟩ merged to [j] 'valle' per hualde2005; trill [r] 'reluce' per alarcos_llorach1994.

sentence: Hizo mucho frío esti iviernu.
ipa: ˈiθo ˈmutʃo ˈfɾio ˈesti iˈbjeɾnu
gloss_en: It was very cold this winter.
notes: ⟨z⟩ [θ] 'Hizo' per penny2002; montañés masculine final -u 'iviernu' — the conservative Cantabrian -o>-u closure — per penny2002; diphthong 'iviernu' [-je-] per hualde2005.

sentence: La vaca pace la yerba nel prau.
ipa: la ˈbaka ˈpaθe la ˈʝeɾba ˈnel ˈpɾaw
gloss_en: The cow grazes the grass in the meadow.
notes: ⟨c⟩ before front vowel [θ] 'pace' per penny2002; yeísmo: ⟨y⟩ [ʝ] 'yerba' with no [ʎ] in the row per hualde2005; montañés falling diphthong 'prau' per alarcos_llorach1994.

sentence: ¿Cuántos años tiene to hermano?
ipa: ˈkuantos̺ ˈaɲos̺ ˈtjene ˈto eˈɾmano
gloss_en: How many years does your brother have?
notes: ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'años' and Castilian final -o 'hermano' per penny2002; diphthong 'tiene' [tje-] per hualde2005.

sentence: El gato negro duerme cabo'l lar.
ipa: el ˈɡato ˈneɡɾo ˈdweɾme ˈkaβo ˈl ˈlaɾ
gloss_en: The black cat sleeps by the hearth.
notes: Castilian velar-frame lect: final -o 'gato','negro' and tonic Ŏ>/ue/ 'duerme' per penny2002; montañés 'lar' (hearth) per alarcos_llorach1994.

sentence: No quiero más pan ni queso.
ipa: ˈno ˈkjeɾo ˈmas̺ ˈpan ni ˈkeso
gloss_en: I don't want more bread or cheese.
notes: Castilian negator 'No'/'ni' (not Asturleonese 'nun'/'nin') and final -o 'queso' per penny2002; diphthong 'quiero' [kje-] per hualde2005.

sentence: La mujer merca cebollas en la plaza.
ipa: la muˈxeɾ ˈmeɾka θeˈβojas̺ en la ˈplaθa
gloss_en: The woman buys onions in the square.
notes: ⟨j⟩ velar [x] 'mujer' — the Castilian frame, contrasting with Asturian ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] — per penny2002; ⟨c⟩/⟨z⟩ [θ] 'cebollas','plaza' per hualde2005; montañés 'mercar' per alarcos_llorach1994.

sentence: ¡Cierra la puerta, que fai frío!
ipa: ˈθjera la ˈpweɾta ke ˈfaj ˈfɾio
gloss_en: Shut the door, it's cold!
notes: ⟨c⟩ before front vowel [θ] 'Cierra' per penny2002; tonic Ĕ>/ie/ 'Cierra', Ŏ>/ue/ 'puerta' and word-initial trill [r] per hualde2005.

sentence: Llovía mucho cuando volvimos a casa.
ipa: joˈbia ˈmutʃo ˈkwando boˈlbimos̺ a ˈkasa
gloss_en: It rained a lot when we came home.
notes: Yeísta ⟨ll⟩ merged to [j] 'Llovía' per penny2002; betacism 'volvimos' [b] and 'mucho' per hualde2005.

sentence: La fuente corre cerca la ilesia.
ipa: la ˈfwente ˈkore ˈθeɾka la iˈlesja
gloss_en: The spring runs near the church.
notes: ⟨c⟩ [θ] 'cerca' per penny2002; tonic Ŏ>/ue/ 'fuente', word-initial trill [r] 'corre' and simplified 'ilesia' per hualde2005.

sentence: El perro ladra toda la nueche.
ipa: el ˈpero ˈladɾa ˈtoða la ˈnwetʃe
gloss_en: The dog barks all night.
notes: Tonic Ŏ>/ue/ 'nueche' per penny2002; trill [r] 'perro' per alarcos_llorach1994; 'nueche' (night) as a montañés/Asturleonese-shared form per penny2002.

sentence: La nena coge una flor amarilla.
ipa: la ˈnena ˈkoxe una ˈfloɾ amaˈɾija
gloss_en: The girl picks a yellow flower.
notes: ⟨g⟩ before front vowel velar [x] 'coge' — the Castilian frame — per penny2002; yeísta ⟨ll⟩ merged to [j] 'amarilla' per hualde2005.

sentence: ¿Ónde está el camino pa la ilesia?
ipa: ˈonde eˈsta el kaˈmino ˈpa la iˈlesja
gloss_en: Where is the way to the church?
notes: Castilian final -o 'camino' per penny2002; simplified 'ilesia' (< iglesia) and dialectal 'ónde' per alarcos_llorach1994.

sentence: Comimos cocido con chorizo.
ipa: koˈmimos̺ koˈθiðo kon tʃoˈɾiθo
gloss_en: We ate stew with chorizo.
notes: ⟨c⟩ before front vowel [θ] 'cocido' per penny2002; ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'chorizo' and Castilian final -o per hualde2005.

sentence: El molín vieju muele'l trigo.
ipa: el moˈlin ˈbjexu ˈmwele ˈl ˈtɾiɣo
gloss_en: The old mill grinds the wheat.
notes: ⟨j⟩ velar [x] 'vieju' (Castilian frame) with montañés final -u per penny2002; tonic Ŏ>/ue/ 'muele' per hualde2005.

sentence: Diez homes trabajan en la mina.
ipa: ˈdjeθ ˈomes̺ tɾaˈβaxan en la ˈmina
gloss_en: Ten men work in the mine.
notes: ⟨z⟩ [θ] 'Diez' per penny2002; ⟨j⟩ velar [x] 'trabajan' — Castilian, vs Asturian ⟨y⟩ [ʝ] 'trabayan' — and diphthong 'Diez' per hualde2005.

sentence: La luna reluce sobre los tejaos.
ipa: la ˈluna reˈluθe ˈsobɾe los̺ ˈtexaos̺
gloss_en: The moon shines over the rooftops.
notes: ⟨c⟩ before front vowel [θ] 'reluce' per penny2002; ⟨j⟩ velar [x] 'tejaos' (< tejados, Castilian frame) and trill [r] per hualde2005.

sentence: La lloría del monte trai agua.
ipa: la joˈɾia del ˈmonte ˈtɾaj ˈaɡa
gloss_en: The mountain spring brings water.
notes: Yeísta ⟨ll⟩ merged to [j] 'lloría' per penny2002; montañés 'lloría' (mountain spring) and 'trai' per alarcos_llorach1994.

sentence: El zagal pela la manzana.
ipa: el θaˈɣal ˈpela la maˈnθana
gloss_en: The lad peels the apple.
notes: ⟨z⟩ [θ] 'zagal','manzana' per penny2002; montañés 'zagal' (lad) and 'pelar' per hualde2005.

sentence: El humu sube pela chimenea.
ipa: el ˈumu ˈsuβe ˈpela tʃiˈmenea
gloss_en: The smoke rises through the chimney.
notes: Montañés masculine final -u 'humu' — the conservative -o>-u closure that sets Cantabrian apart from standard Castilian -o — per penny2002; ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'chimenea' per hualde2005.
